ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "satisfaction_level" REAL /* Satisfaction level of the employee */,
  "last_evaluation" REAL /* Last performance evaluation score */,
  "number_project" INTEGER /* Number of projects the employee is involved in */,
  "average_monthly_hours" INTEGER /* Average monthly working hours */,
  "time_spent_company" INTEGER /* Years spent in the company */,
  "work_accident" INTEGER /* Indicator if the employee had a work accident */,
  "promotion_last_5years" INTEGER /* Indicator if the employee was promoted in the last 5 years */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the employee belongs to */,
  "salary" TEXT /* Salary category of the employee */,
  "left" INTEGER /* Indicator if the employee has left the company */,
  "employee_name" TEXT /* Name of the employee */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the employee */,
  "hiring_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  "termination_date" TEXT /* Date the employee left the company, if applicable */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager overseeing the employee */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Current employment status of the employee */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location or office where the employee works */,
  "email" TEXT /* Company email of the employee */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_salary FOREIGN KEY ("salary") REFERENCES salaries ("salary_range")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location or office where the department is based */,
  "budget" REAL /* Annual budget allocated to the department */,
  "headcount" INTEGER /* Number of employees in the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salaries" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary range */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Description of the salary range */,
  "min_salary" REAL /* Minimum salary in the range */,
  "max_salary" REAL /* Maximum salary in the range */,
  "average_salary" REAL /* Average salary in the range */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance review */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being reviewed */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date of the performance review */,
  "review_score" REAL /* Score given during the performance review */,
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager who conducted the review */,
  "reviewer_name" TEXT /* Name of the manager who conducted the review */,
  "review_comments" TEXT /* Detailed comments from the performance review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reviews_reviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("reviewer_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reviews_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "work_accidents" (
  "accident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each work accident */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee involved in the accident */,
  "accident_date" TEXT /* Date of the work accident */,
  "incident_description" TEXT /* Description of the incident */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the accident */,
  "injured_body_part" TEXT /* Body part that was injured */,
  "recovery_days" INTEGER /* Number of days the employee took to recover */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost associated with the accident */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("accident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_work_accidents_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "promotions" (
  "promotion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each promotion */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who was promoted */,
  "promotion_date" TEXT /* Date of the promotion */,
  "new_position" TEXT /* New position of the employee after promotion */,
  "previous_position" TEXT /* Position the employee held before the promotion */,
  "promotion_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the promotion */,
  "promotion_effective_date" TEXT /* Date the promotion became effective */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("promotion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_promotions_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments_managers" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager who oversees the department */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the manager started overseeing the department */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the manager stopped overseeing the department, if applicable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id", "manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "managers" (
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each manager */,
  "manager_name" TEXT /* Name of the manager */,
  "email" TEXT /* Company email of the manager */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the manager */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the manager */,
  "hiring_date" TEXT /* Date the manager was hired */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_exit_surveys" (
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each exit survey */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who completed the survey */,
  "survey_date" TEXT /* Date the survey was completed */,
  "reason_for_leaving" TEXT /* Reason given for leaving the company */,
  "satisfaction_level" REAL /* Satisfaction level when leaving the company */,
  "last_position" TEXT /* Last position held by the employee before leaving */,
  "last_salary" TEXT /* Last salary of the employee before leaving */,
  "recommends_company" INTEGER /* Whether the employee would recommend the company to others */,
  "suggestions_for_improvement" TEXT /* Any suggestions the employee has for improving the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("survey_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_exit_surveys_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_training_sessions" (
  "training_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each training session */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who attended the training */,
  "training_date" TEXT /* Date of the training session */,
  "training_topic" TEXT /* Topic of the training session */,
  "trainer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the trainer who conducted the session */,
  "trainer_name" TEXT /* Name of the trainer who conducted the session */,
  "training_duration_hours" INTEGER /* Duration of the training session in hours */,
  "training_feedback" TEXT /* Feedback from the employee on the training session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("training_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_training_sessions_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_training_sessions_trainer_id FOREIGN KEY ("trainer_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee giving feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date the feedback was provided */,
  "feedback_text" TEXT /* Text of the feedback */,
  "feedback_type" TEXT /* Type of feedback (e.g., positive, negative) */,
  "suggested_actions" TEXT /* Any suggested actions based on the feedback */,
  "follow_up_required" INTEGER /* Indicator whether follow-up is required based on the feedback */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_feedback_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
